evidences.title: The beginner's guide to the 2026 F1 calendar
evidences.content: Our guide explains the F1 calendar, when the season starts and which races are on it. Australia (6-8 March) hosts the first Grand Prix of the year, before the paddock visits China (13-15 March) – the first F1 Sprint weekend of 2026. A better geographical flow of races (compared to historic seasons) continues, with Japan (27-29 March) hosting the third event of the season, before the Bahrain (10-12 April) and Saudi Arabian (17-19 April) Grands Prix take place on back-to-back weekends. The 2026 Formula 1 season concludes with a triple header, with the Las Vegas Grand Prix (19-21 November), Qatar Grand Prix (27-29 November) and Abu Dhabi Grand Prix (4-6 December) wrapping up the year. ### How many races are on the 2026 F1 calendar? The 2026 Formula 1 calendar features 24 Grand Prix weekends, including six F1 Sprint races, taking place around the world from March through December. Six circuits will host F1 Sprint weekends in 2026 – the fifth season of the Sprint format.

evidences.title: EXPLAINED: Why Formula 1 race starts will be different in 2026
evidences.content: Race starts under the sweeping new technical regulations for 2026 are set to be slightly different to what we’ve seen before in F1. Because of the way the new cars’ power units are configured, the sequence of actions performed by the drivers as they approach the grid, wait for the start and then accelerate away has become a little different – as was demonstrated during pre-season testing. The challenge for drivers will be to balance the engine revs – alongside the time needed to get the turbo up to speed – and then to hold the target revs intended to match the release of the clutch for the best start performance. ### What is the new race start procedure? Following discussions on this topic, the FIA confirmed that it would trial a new race start process – featuring a pre-start warning – during the second pre-season test in Bahrain, which is set to be replicated from Australia onwards.

evidences.title: Formula 1 rumored to cancel two races due to the USA ...
evidences.content: # Formula 1 rumored to cancel two races due to the USA, Iran war in 2026. Formula 1 is likely to cancel the races at Bahrain and Saudi Arabia due to the ongoing war between the United States and Iran, as reported by Crash.net's Adam Cooper. If Formula 1 were to cancel Bahrain and Saudi Arabia, it would be unlikely for the races to be replaced, according to Cooper. This is a very disappointing possibility, but the war between the United States and Iran would make it unsafe to hold races in Bahrain and Saudi Arabia. The war could end by the time Formula 1 is scheduled to come into town, but it wouldn't be a smart decision. Formula 1 appears likely for two cancellations and a smaller season than expected. *This article originally appeared on Motorsports Wire: Formula 1 rumored to cancel two races due to the USA, Iran war in 2026*.

evidences.title: Carlos Sainz concludes his first season as a Williams driver
evidences.content: Sunday, 7 December 2025: - Race: P13, 58 laps Carlos Sainz finished thirteenth in the Abu Dhabi GP, where the grid was split between a one-stop strategy and a two-stopper. The Williams race driver, starting from twelfth on the grid, extended his first stint on the Medium tyres until Lap 18. The Spanish gently took care of his tyres throughout the second stint and made them last until the end. He was running in eleventh place with five laps to go, but ultimately tyre degradation cost him several places against other cars in fresher rubber. Carlos concludes his first season as a Williams race driver with two podiums, the ninth place of the Drivers’ Championship and a crucial contribution to his team’s fifth place in the Constructors’ standings. [...] Dec 7, 2025 ## Carlos Sainz concludes his first season as a Williams driver 1. News /2. Carlos Sainz concludes his first season as a Williams driver Sunday, 7 December 2025: - Race: P13, 58 laps [...] "We need to stay hungry and continue to push all together. We have built solid foundations for next year but there is always margin for improvement. Thanks to the entire team for their effort and to every single one of our fans out there...
